etymology.description: semantic 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic 令(令) (lìng) -> medicinal plant
etymology.interpretation: 苓(líng) combines 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") as the semantic component with 令(令) (lìng) as the phonetic component to signify a plant used in medicine, specifically referring to poria, a type of fungus with medicinal properties.

examples.chinese: 苓是一种药用植物(苓是一種藥用植物)。
examples.pinyin: Líng shì yī zhǒng yàoyòng zhíwù.
examples.english: Poria is a type of medicinal plant.

examples.chinese: 许多人用苓来调理身体(許多人用苓來調理身體)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén yòng líng lái tiáolǐ shēntǐ.
examples.english: Many people use poria to regulate their bodies.

examples.chinese: 这种草药中含有苓和其他成分(這種草藥中含有苓和其他成分)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng cǎoyào zhōng hányǒu líng hé qítā chéngfèn.
examples.english: This herbal medicine contains poria and other ingredients.
